cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the answer. The question is asking what the author did when he met Mr. Lee. The passage starts with the author walking down the street with his mother, who stops to speak to Mr. Lee. The author says he knew he could see Mr. Lee anytime around the neighborhood, so he didn't pay any attention to him. That part is key here. The key sentence is: "I didn't pay any attention to him." So when the author met Mr. Lee, he didn't engage or acknowledge him. The mother later scolds him for not speaking, using the example of a dog wagging its tail. So the author's action was to ignore Mr. Lee. Looking at the options: (A) He talked with somebody else. But the story doesn't mention him talking to someone else instead.
